assumptions: No prior Wise Platform account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.

first_success.outcome: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.
first_success.observable_signal: The API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of Wise profiles.

route_selection.surface: Wise Business personal API token + cURL GET /v1/profiles
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Wise Business personal API token + cURL GET /v1/profiles

candidates.name: Wise Business personal API token + cURL GET /v1/profiles
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to the Wise Business account used for API access.
Create a personal API token in the documented Wise settings flow and copy it securely.
required_path.observable_result: A personal API token is available.

required_path.action: Send the documented authenticated cURL GET request to /v1/profiles with the personal token.
Confirm the response is HTTP 200 and contains the account's profile objects.
required_path.observable_result: A profiles JSON array is visible.

prerequisites.description: A Wise Business account eligible to create a personal API token.

external_gates.description: Personal API tokens are for eligible Wise Business customers and are not the partner OAuth path.
The bearer token must be protected because it grants access to the associated Wise account.

platform_outcomes.description: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Call GET /v1/profiles
evidence_supported: Wise accepts a Business personal API token and returns the authenticated account's profiles from GET /v1/profiles.
The API returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of Wise profiles.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Call GET /v1/profiles path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
